message:
docs: add Astro Starlight documentation site

Restructure existing README and CLAUDE.md content into a 6-page docs
site at docs/ using @astrojs/starlight. Pages: overview, getting
started, SDK reference, color presets, architecture, and deployment.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";
+import starlight from "@astrojs/starlight";
+
+export default defineConfig({
+  integrations: [
+    starlight({
+      title: "drawmode",
+      social: [
+        {
+          icon: "github",
+          label: "GitHub",
+          href: "https://github.com/teamchong/drawmode",
+        },
+      ],
+      sidebar: [
+        { label: "Overview", slug: "index" },
+        { label: "Getting Started", slug: "getting-started" },
+        { label: "SDK Reference", slug: "sdk-reference" },
+        { label: "Color Presets", slug: "color-presets" },
+        { label: "Architecture", slug: "architecture" },
+        { label: "Deployment", slug: "deployment" },
+      ],
+    }),
+  ],
+});

@@ -0,0 +1,132 @@
+---
+title: Architecture
+description: How drawmode works — execution pipeline, layout engine, and WASM validation.
+---
+
+## Code Mode Pattern
+
+drawmode exposes a single `draw` MCP tool. The LLM writes TypeScript code against the Diagram SDK:
+
+1. LLM receives one tool (`draw`) with SDK type definitions (~100 lines)
+2. LLM writes code using the `Diagram` class
+3. `executor.ts` runs code via `new Function("Diagram", code)` with a `ConfiguredDiagram` subclass
+4. Graphviz (`@hpcc-js/wasm-graphviz`) handles layout positioning and arrow routing via the `dot` engine
+5. Zig WASM handles validation (layout fallback if Graphviz unavailable)
+6. Output: `.excalidraw` file, excalidraw.com URL, `.png` file, or `.svg` file
+
+## Project Structure
+
+```
+drawmode/
+├── src/                     # TypeScript (MCP server + SDK)
+│   ├── index.ts             # MCP server entry point (stdio + HTTP)
+│   ├── sdk.ts               # Diagram SDK (addBox, connect, render, etc.)
+│   ├── executor.ts          # Local executor (new Function + Diagram subclass)
+│   ├── layout.ts            # Layout bridge (Graphviz primary, Zig WASM fallback)
+│   ├── upload.ts            # Excalidraw.com upload (encrypt + POST)
+│   ├── png.ts               # Image export (PNG/SVG via puppeteer + Excalidraw CDN)
+│   ├── types.ts             # Shared types (ColorPreset, ShapeOpts, ConnectOpts, etc.)
+│   └── widget.html          # MCP Apps HTML widget for Claude Desktop/Cowork
+├── wasm/                    # Zig WASM module
+│   ├── src/
+│   │   ├── main.zig         # WASM exports (alloc, resetHeap, layoutGraph, routeArrows, validate)
+│   │   ├── layout.zig       # Grid layout fallback
+│   │   ├── arrows.zig       # Arrow routing (unused — Graphviz handles routing)
+│   │   ├── validate.zig     # Structural validation
+│   │   └── util.zig         # Shared utilities
+│   └── build.zig
+├── worker/                  # Cloudflare Worker entry point (remote MCP)
+│   ├── index.ts
+│   └── wrangler.toml
+├── package.json
+└── tsconfig.json
+```
+
+## Layout Engine: Graphviz
+
+The primary layout engine is Graphviz via `@hpcc-js/wasm-graphviz` — the real Graphviz C library compiled to WASM.
+
+- **`dot` layout**: Layered graph layout (Sugiyama algorithm) with proper crossing minimization
+- **`splines=ortho`**: Orthogonal edge routing (90-degree elbows matching Excalidraw style), falls back to `splines=polyline` if ortho fails
+- **Cluster subgraphs**: Groups rendered as Graphviz clusters for proper containment
+- **`rank=same`**: Nodes with same `row` value share a rank in the layout
+- **Coordinate conversion**: Graphviz points (Y-up) → Excalidraw pixels (Y-down)
+
+### Fallback Chain
+
+```
+Graphviz → Zig WASM grid → TypeScript grid
+```
+
+If Graphviz is unavailable, layout falls back to the Zig WASM grid layout module, then to a pure TypeScript grid implementation.
+
+## Zig WASM Module
+
+The Zig WASM module handles validation and provides a grid layout fallback:
+
+**WASM exports:**
+
+| Export | Description |
+|--------|-------------|
+| `layoutGraph` | Grid layout fallback (when Graphviz unavailable) |
+| `routeArrows` | Arrow routing (exists but unused — Graphviz handles routing) |
+| `validate` | Structural validation (bound text, duplicate IDs, overlapping elements) |
+
+Input/output is JSON bytes — the TypeScript layer serializes to JSON, passes to WASM, and reads back results.
+
+### Validation Checks
+
+- Bound text elements have matching containers
+- No duplicate element IDs
+- Arrow endpoints reference valid elements
+- No overlapping elements
+
+## Excalidraw JSON Internals
+
+The SDK handles these Excalidraw quirks so the LLM doesn't have to:
+
+- Labels need **two elements**: shape with `boundElements` + text with `containerId`
+- The `label` property does NOT work in raw JSON
+- Elbow arrows need: `elbowed: true`, `roundness: null`, `roughness: 0`
+- Arrow x,y must be on source shape edge, not center
+- Multiple arrows from same edge must be staggered (20%, 35%, 50%, 65%, 80%)
+
+## Image Export Pipeline
+
+PNG and SVG export uses headless Chrome (puppeteer) to load the Excalidraw library from CDN and call `ExcalidrawLib.exportToSvg()` — the same rendering pipeline as the Excalidraw web app.
+
+- **PNG**: SVG → canvas at 2x resolution → `toDataURL("image/png")`
+- **SVG**: Direct `XMLSerializer.serializeToString()` of the SVG element
+- **CDN**: Pinned to `@excalidraw/excalidraw@0.17.6`
+- **Optional**: `puppeteer` is in `optionalDependencies` — PNG/SVG export gracefully fails if not installed
+
+![Architecture diagram](/architecture.png)
+
+## Dependencies
+
+### TypeScript
+
+| Package | Purpose |
+|---------|---------|
+| `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` | MCP protocol (McpServer, transports) |
+| `@hpcc-js/wasm-graphviz` | Graphviz C library compiled to WASM |
+| `zod` | Schema validation for MCP tool parameters |
+| `puppeteer` (optional) | Headless Chrome for PNG/SVG export |
+
+### Zig
+
+No external dependencies. Standard library only.
+
+## Development
+
+```bash
+pnpm install              # Install dependencies
+pnpm build                # Build TS + WASM (Zig failure non-fatal)
+pnpm build:wasm           # Build WASM module only
+pnpm dev                  # Dev server (HTTP mode)
+pnpm test                 # Run tests
+pnpm typecheck            # TypeScript type checking
+
+cd wasm && zig build       # Build WASM module only
+cd wasm && zig build test  # Run Zig tests
+```

@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@
+---
+title: Color Presets
+description: Semantic color presets with hex values for diagram elements.
+---
+
+drawmode includes 10 general-purpose semantic color presets and 18 cloud provider presets. Use the `color` option on `addBox`, `addEllipse`, or `addDiamond`.
+
+```typescript
+d.addBox("API Server", { color: "backend" });
+d.addBox("Lambda", { color: "aws-compute" });
+```
+
+You can also override colors directly with `strokeColor` and `backgroundColor` hex values.
+
+## General Presets
+
+| Preset | Use for | Background | Stroke |
+|--------|---------|------------|--------|
+| `frontend` | UI, browser, React | `#a5d8ff` | `#1971c2` |
+| `backend` | APIs, services, servers | `#d0bfff` | `#7048e8` |
+| `database` | Postgres, MySQL, DynamoDB | `#b2f2bb` | `#2f9e44` |
+| `storage` | S3, R2, blob storage | `#ffec99` | `#f08c00` |
+| `ai` | ML models, embeddings | `#e599f7` | `#9c36b5` |
+| `external` | Third-party APIs | `#ffc9c9` | `#e03131` |
+| `orchestration` | K8s, Docker, schedulers | `#ffa8a8` | `#c92a2a` |
+| `queue` | Kafka, SQS, RabbitMQ | `#fff3bf` | `#fab005` |
+| `cache` | Redis, Memcached | `#ffe8cc` | `#fd7e14` |
+| `users` | End users, actors | `#e7f5ff` | `#1971c2` |
+
+## AWS Presets
+
+| Preset | Background | Stroke |
+|--------|------------|--------|
+| `aws-compute` | `#FF9900` | `#C27400` |
+| `aws-storage` | `#3F8624` | `#2D6119` |
+| `aws-database` | `#3B48CC` | `#2C3699` |
+| `aws-network` | `#8C4FFF` | `#6B3DBF` |
+| `aws-security` | `#DD344C` | `#A52739` |
+| `aws-ml` | `#01A88D` | `#017D69` |
+
+## Azure Presets
+
+| Preset | Background | Stroke |
+|--------|------------|--------|
+| `azure-compute` | `#0078D4` | `#005BA1` |
+| `azure-data` | `#50E6FF` | `#3CB8CC` |
+| `azure-network` | `#773ADC` | `#5A2CA5` |
+| `azure-ai` | `#0078D4` | `#005BA1` |
+
+## GCP Presets
+
+| Preset | Background | Stroke |
+|--------|------------|--------|
+| `gcp-compute` | `#4285F4` | `#3267B8` |
+| `gcp-data` | `#34A853` | `#27803E` |
+| `gcp-network` | `#FBBC04` | `#C99603` |
+| `gcp-ai` | `#EA4335` | `#B23228` |
+
+## Kubernetes Presets
+
+| Preset | Background | Stroke |
+|--------|------------|--------|
+| `k8s-pod` | `#326CE5` | `#264FAB` |
+| `k8s-service` | `#5B9BD5` | `#4577A0` |
+| `k8s-ingress` | `#00BCD4` | `#0097A7` |
+| `k8s-volume` | `#FFC107` | `#C99605` |

@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+---
+title: Deployment
+description: Deploy drawmode locally or to Cloudflare Workers.
+---
+
+## Local (stdio)
+
+For Claude Code, Claude Desktop, and Cursor — the MCP server communicates over stdio:
+
+```bash
+npx drawmode --stdio
+```
+
+Configure your client:
+
+```json
+{
+  "mcpServers": {
+    "drawmode": {
+      "command": "npx",
+      "args": ["drawmode", "--stdio"]
+    }
+  }
+}
+```
+
+## Local (HTTP)
+
+Streamable HTTP server on port 3001:
+
+```bash
+npx drawmode
+```
+
+Useful for development and testing with HTTP-based MCP clients.
+
+## Cloudflare Workers
+
+Deploy the `worker/` directory to Cloudflare Workers for remote MCP access:
+
+[![Deploy to Cloudflare](https://deploy.workers.cloudflare.com/button)](https://deploy.workers.cloudflare.com/?url=https://github.com/teamchong/drawmode/tree/main/worker)
+
+### Manual Deployment
+
+```bash
+cd worker
+npx wrangler deploy
+```
+
+### Worker Requirements
+
+The Worker requires these compatibility flags in `wrangler.toml`:
+
+- `nodejs_compat` — Node.js API compatibility
+- `unsafe_eval` — required for `new Function()` code execution
+
+### PNG Export on Workers
+
+The Worker supports PNG export via [Cloudflare Browser Rendering](https://developers.cloudflare.com/browser-rendering/) — headless Chromium on the edge renders pixel-perfect PNGs using the Excalidraw renderer.
+
+- Free tier includes 10 minutes/day of browser rendering time
+- For local dev with browser rendering: `cd worker && npx wrangler dev --remote`
+
+## PNG/SVG Export (Local)
+
+Local PNG and SVG export requires [puppeteer](https://pptr.dev/):
+
+```bash
+npm install puppeteer
+```
+
+puppeteer is listed in `optionalDependencies` — if not installed, PNG/SVG export gracefully fails and other formats continue to work.
+
+The export pipeline:
+1. Launches headless Chrome via puppeteer
+2. Loads `@excalidraw/excalidraw@0.17.6` from CDN
+3. Calls `ExcalidrawLib.exportToSvg()` with the diagram elements
+4. **PNG**: Renders SVG to canvas at 2x resolution, exports as PNG
+5. **SVG**: Serializes the SVG element directly
+
+## Publishing
+
+### npm
+
+```bash
+npm publish
+```
+
+Users install via `npx drawmode`.
+
+### MCP Registry
+
+Publish to `registry.modelcontextprotocol.io` for auto-indexing by PulseMCP and Smithery.

@@ -0,0 +1,100 @@
+---
+title: Getting Started
+description: Install drawmode and generate your first diagram.
+---
+
+## Installation
+
+drawmode runs as an MCP server. No separate install is needed — `npx` handles it.
+
+```bash
+# Claude Code / Cursor (stdio mode)
+npx drawmode --stdio
+
+# HTTP mode (Streamable HTTP on port 3001)
+npx drawmode
+```
+
+## Client Configuration
+
+### Claude Code
+
+Add to your Claude Code MCP settings:
+
+```json
+{
+  "mcpServers": {
+    "drawmode": {
+      "command": "npx",
+      "args": ["drawmode", "--stdio"]
+    }
+  }
+}
+```
+
+### Claude Desktop
+
+Add the same config to Claude Desktop's MCP settings file:
+
+- **macOS**: `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
+- **Windows**: `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`
+
+```json
+{
+  "mcpServers": {
+    "drawmode": {
+      "command": "npx",
+      "args": ["drawmode", "--stdio"]
+    }
+  }
+}
+```
+
+### Cursor
+
+Add to Cursor's MCP configuration:
+
+```json
+{
+  "mcpServers": {
+    "drawmode": {
+      "command": "npx",
+      "args": ["drawmode", "--stdio"]
+    }
+  }
+}
+```
+
+## Your First Diagram
+
+Once configured, ask your LLM to draw a diagram. The LLM receives a single `draw` tool and writes TypeScript code against the Diagram SDK:
+
+```typescript
+const d = new Diagram();
+const web = d.addBox("Web App", { row: 0, col: 0, color: "frontend" });
+const api = d.addBox("API Server", { row: 0, col: 1, color: "backend" });
+const db = d.addBox("Database", { row: 1, col: 1, color: "database" });
+d.connect(web, api, "REST");
+d.connect(api, db, "queries");
+return d.render({ format: "url" });
+```
+
+This produces a shareable excalidraw.com URL with the rendered diagram.
+
+## Output Formats
+
+| Format | Description | Works in |
+|--------|-------------|----------|
+| `excalidraw` | `.excalidraw` JSON file | Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code |
+| `url` | Shareable excalidraw.com link | All clients |
+| `png` | PNG image (requires puppeteer) | Local with puppeteer, Cloudflare Worker |
+| `svg` | SVG markup | Local with puppeteer |
+
+## How It Works
+
+1. LLM receives one tool (`draw`) with TypeScript type definitions
+2. LLM writes code against the `Diagram` SDK
+3. Executor runs the code — SDK handles labels, colors, IDs
+4. Graphviz `dot` engine handles layout positioning and edge routing
+5. Zig WASM handles validation
+6. Output: `.excalidraw` file, excalidraw.com URL, PNG, or SVG

@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@
+---
+title: drawmode
+description: Code Mode MCP server for generating Excalidraw architecture diagrams.
+template: splash
+hero:
+  tagline: LLMs write TypeScript, not raw JSON. One tool, typed SDK, zero prompt engineering.
+  actions:
+    - text: Get Started
+      link: /getting-started/
+      icon: right-arrow
+      variant: primary
+    - text: SDK Reference
+      link: /sdk-reference/
+      variant: minimal
+---
+
+import { Card, CardGrid } from "@astrojs/starlight/components";
+
+## Features
+
+<CardGrid>
+  <Card title="Code Mode" icon="pencil">
+    LLM writes ~10 lines of TypeScript against a typed SDK. The SDK handles all Excalidraw complexity — labels, bindings, arrow math.
+  </Card>
+  <Card title="Automatic Layout" icon="setting">
+    Graphviz `dot` engine (Sugiyama algorithm) with crossing minimization and orthogonal edge routing.
+  </Card>
+  <Card title="28 Color Presets" icon="seti:color">
+    10 semantic presets + 18 cloud provider presets for AWS, Azure, GCP, and Kubernetes.
+  </Card>
+  <Card title="Multiple Outputs" icon="document">
+    `.excalidraw` files, shareable excalidraw.com URLs, PNG images, and SVG exports.
+  </Card>
+  <Card title="Edit Existing Diagrams" icon="pen">
+    `Diagram.fromFile()` loads `.excalidraw` files for modification — find, update, and remove nodes.
+  </Card>
+  <Card title="Deploy Anywhere" icon="rocket">
+    Local stdio, local HTTP, or Cloudflare Workers for remote MCP access.
+  </Card>
+</CardGrid>
+
+## Why Code Mode?
+
+Traditional MCP diagram tools ask the LLM to produce raw Excalidraw JSON — hundreds of lines with pixel coordinates, bound text element pairs, arrow binding math, and edge routing. This is fragile and error-prone.
+
+drawmode flips the approach: the LLM writes **~10 lines of TypeScript** against a typed SDK. The SDK handles all the Excalidraw complexity (labels need two elements, arrows need binding math, elbow routing needs specific flags). Graphviz handles layout. The result is always valid.
+
+```
+Traditional:  LLM → 500 lines of JSON → broken diagrams
+drawmode:     LLM → 10 lines of TypeScript → SDK + Graphviz → valid diagrams
+```
+
+## Quick Example
+
+```typescript
+const d = new Diagram();
+const api = d.addBox("API Gateway", { row: 0, col: 1, color: "backend" });
+const db = d.addBox("Postgres", { row: 1, col: 0, color: "database" });
+const cache = d.addBox("Redis", { row: 1, col: 2, color: "cache" });
+d.connect(api, db, "queries");
+d.connect(api, cache, "reads", { style: "dashed" });
+d.addGroup("Data Layer", [db, cache]);
+return d.render({ format: "url" });
+```

@@ -0,0 +1,256 @@
+---
+title: SDK Reference
+description: Complete API reference for the Diagram SDK.
+---
+
+The `Diagram` class is the main interface for creating diagrams. All methods return element IDs that can be used with `connect()`, `addGroup()`, and editing methods.
+
+## Creating Elements
+
+### `addBox(label, opts?)`
+
+Add a rectangle element. Returns the element ID.
+
+```typescript
+const id = d.addBox("API Gateway", { row: 0, col: 1, color: "backend" });
+```
+
+### `addEllipse(label, opts?)`
+
+Add an ellipse element. Returns the element ID.
+
+```typescript
+const id = d.addEllipse("Start", { row: 0, col: 0, color: "users" });
+```
+
+### `addDiamond(label, opts?)`
+
+Add a diamond shape (for flowchart decisions). Returns the element ID.
+
+```typescript
+const id = d.addDiamond("Is Valid?", { row: 1, col: 1, color: "orchestration" });
+```
+
+### `addText(text, opts?)`
+
+Add standalone text (no container). Returns the element ID.
+
+```typescript
+const id = d.addText("System Overview", {
+  x: 100, y: 50,
+  fontSize: 24,
+  fontFamily: 2, // Helvetica
+});
+```
+
+Text options:
+| Option | Type | Description |
+|--------|------|-------------|
+| `x` | `number` | Absolute x position |
+| `y` | `number` | Absolute y position |
+| `fontSize` | `number` | Font size (default 16) |
+| `fontFamily` | `1 \| 2 \| 3` | Virgil / Helvetica / Cascadia |
+| `color` | `ColorPreset` | Semantic color preset |
+| `strokeColor` | `string` | Hex color override |
+
+### `addLine(points, opts?)`
+
+Add a line element. Returns the element ID.
+
+```typescript
+const id = d.addLine([[0, 0], [200, 100]], {
+  strokeColor: "#e03131",
+  strokeWidth: 2,
+  strokeStyle: "dashed",
+});
+```
+
+## Grouping
+
+### `addGroup(label, children)`
+
+Group elements with a dashed boundary. Returns the group ID.
+
+```typescript
+const db = d.addBox("Postgres", { row: 1, col: 0, color: "database" });
+const cache = d.addBox("Redis", { row: 1, col: 1, color: "cache" });
+d.addGroup("Data Layer", [db, cache]);
+```
+
+### `addFrame(name, children)`
+
+Add a native Excalidraw frame container. Returns the frame ID.
+
+```typescript
+const svc1 = d.addBox("Service A", { color: "backend" });
+const svc2 = d.addBox("Service B", { color: "backend" });
+d.addFrame("Microservices", [svc1, svc2]);
+```
+
+### `removeGroup(id)` / `removeFrame(id)`
+
+Remove a group or frame container. Children are kept.
+
+## Connections
+
+### `connect(from, to, label?, opts?)`
+
+Connect two elements with an arrow.
+
+```typescript
+d.connect(api, db, "queries");
+d.connect(api, cache, "reads", { style: "dashed" });
+d.connect(a, b, "flow", { startArrowhead: "dot", endArrowhead: "triangle" });
+```
+
+## Editing Existing Diagrams
+
+### `Diagram.fromFile(path)`
+
+Load an existing `.excalidraw` file for modification.
+
+```typescript
+const d = await Diagram.fromFile("diagram.excalidraw");
+```
+
+### `findByLabel(label, opts?)`
+
+Find node IDs by label match. Substring match by default, exact with `opts.exact`.
+
+```typescript
+const ids = d.findByLabel("API");           // substring match
+const ids = d.findByLabel("API", { exact: true }); // exact match
+```
+
+### `getNodes()`
+
+Get all node IDs.
+
+```typescript
+const allNodes = d.getNodes();
+```
+
+### `getEdges()`
+
+Get all edges.
+
+```typescript
+const edges = d.getEdges();
+// [{ from: "id1", to: "id2", label: "queries" }, ...]
+```
+
+### `updateNode(id, opts)`
+
+Update a node's properties.
+
+```typescript
+d.updateNode(ids[0], { label: "New API", color: "ai" });
+```
+
+### `updateEdge(from, to, update, matchLabel?)`
+
+Update an existing edge's properties. Optional `matchLabel` disambiguates when there are multiple edges between the same nodes.
+
+```typescript
+d.updateEdge(api, db, { label: "writes", style: "dashed" });
+```
+
+### `removeNode(id)`
+
+Remove a node and its connected edges.
+
+```typescript
+d.removeNode(d.findByLabel("Old Service")[0]);
+```
+
+### `removeEdge(from, to, label?)`
+
+Remove an edge between two nodes. Optional label disambiguates multi-edges.
+
+```typescript
+d.removeEdge(api, db, "queries");
+```
+
+## Rendering
+
+### `render(opts?)`
+
+Render the diagram. Always return this from your code.
+
+```typescript
+return d.render();                              // default: excalidraw JSON
+return d.render({ format: "url" });             // shareable URL
+return d.render({ format: "excalidraw", path: "out.excalidraw" }); // write file
+return d.render({ format: "png", path: "out.png" });  // PNG image
+return d.render({ format: "svg", path: "out.svg" });  // SVG markup
+```
+
+**Render options:**
+| Option | Type | Default | Description |
+|--------|------|---------|-------------|
+| `format` | `"excalidraw" \| "url" \| "png" \| "svg"` | `"excalidraw"` | Output format |
+| `path` | `string` | — | File path to write output |
+
+**Return type (`RenderResult`):**
+| Field | Type | Description |
+|-------|------|-------------|
+| `json` | `object` | Raw Excalidraw JSON |
+| `url` | `string?` | excalidraw.com shareable link |
+| `filePath` | `string?` | Local file path if written |
+| `pngBase64` | `string?` | Base64-encoded PNG (format="png") |
+| `svgString` | `string?` | SVG markup string (format="svg") |
+
+## Type Reference
+
+### `ShapeOpts`
+
+Options for `addBox`, `addEllipse`, and `addDiamond`:
+
+| Option | Type | Default | Description |
+|--------|------|---------|-------------|
+| `row` | `number` | — | Grid row position |
+| `col` | `number` | — | Grid column position |
+| `color` | `ColorPreset` | — | Semantic color preset |
+| `width` | `number` | — | Element width |
+| `height` | `number` | — | Element height |
+| `x` | `number` | — | Absolute x position (bypasses grid) |
+| `y` | `number` | — | Absolute y position (bypasses grid) |
+| `strokeColor` | `string` | — | Hex stroke color override |
+| `backgroundColor` | `string` | — | Hex background color override |
+| `fillStyle` | `"solid" \| "hachure" \| "cross-hatch" \| "zigzag"` | `"solid"` | Fill style |
+| `strokeWidth` | `number` | `2` | Stroke width |
+| `strokeStyle` | `"solid" \| "dashed" \| "dotted"` | `"solid"` | Stroke style |
+| `roughness` | `number` | — | 0=architect, 1=artist, 2=cartoonist |
+| `opacity` | `number` | — | 0-100 |
+| `roundness` | `{ type: number } \| null` | — | Corner roundness |
+| `fontSize` | `number` | `16` | Label font size |
+| `fontFamily` | `1 \| 2 \| 3` | `1` | Virgil / Helvetica / Cascadia |
+| `textAlign` | `"left" \| "center" \| "right"` | — | Text alignment |
+| `verticalAlign` | `"top" \| "middle"` | — | Vertical alignment |
+| `link` | `string \| null` | — | Hyperlink URL |
+| `customData` | `Record<string, unknown> \| null` | — | Arbitrary metadata |
+
+### `ConnectOpts`
+
+Options for `connect()`:
+
+| Option | Type | Default | Description |
+|--------|------|---------|-------------|
+| `style` | `"solid" \| "dashed" \| "dotted"` | `"solid"` | Stroke style |
+| `strokeColor` | `string` | — | Hex stroke color |
+| `strokeWidth` | `number` | — | Stroke width |
+| `roughness` | `number` | — | Roughness level |
+| `opacity` | `number` | — | 0-100 |
+| `startArrowhead` | `Arrowhead` | `null` | Start arrowhead type |
+| `endArrowhead` | `Arrowhead` | `"arrow"` | End arrowhead type |
+| `elbowed` | `boolean` | `true` | Use elbow routing |
+| `labelFontSize` | `number` | — | Label font size |
+| `customData` | `Record<string, unknown> \| null` | — | Arbitrary metadata |
+
+### `Arrowhead`
+
+`null | "arrow" | "bar" | "dot" | "triangle" | "diamond" | "diamond_outline"`
+
+### `ColorPreset`
+
+See [Color Presets](/color-presets/) for the full list with hex values.
